title: United States debates how to address Chinese artificial intelligence as pressure grows not to block it
summary: Startup founders and NVIDIA CEO call for maintaining access to Chinese open models, while the White House evaluates its strategy.

title: NVIDIA raises memory prices and GeForce RTX GPUs will be more expensive
summary: The increase in graphics memory kits will affect prices of future consumer graphics cards.

title: Google allows logging in with a selfie video and recovering blocked accounts
summary: The new biometric verification function offers an alternative to access and recover Google accounts.

title: Geekbench 7 is updated with real CPU tests, AI benchmarks and CUDA support
summary: The new version of the popular benchmark includes media workloads, games and artificial intelligence tests.

title: Intel and AMD raise server CPU prices by 40% in agreements with China
summary: The manufacturers sign long-term contracts with Chinese customers that guarantee purchase volumes without setting definitive prices.

title: Framework doubles the price of memory in its Laptop 13 Pro laptops due to the high cost of LPCAMM2 modules
summary: The company modifies memory configurations of pre-orders to face supplier price increases.

title: PlayStation 6 controller will incorporate magnetic joysticks to eliminate 'drifting'
summary: Sony will adopt TMR sensors in analog sticks to solve the wear that causes involuntary drifts.

title: Microsoft tests free Xbox Cloud Gaming with ads and one-hour sessions
summary: The streaming gaming service offers free access in exchange for advertising, limited to 60-minute sessions.

title: Meta launches an optimistic AI ad with a song about the end of the world
summary: The advertising campaign seeks to promote Meta's artificial intelligence while generating controversy over the musical choice.

title: Big tech companies accumulate a hidden debt of 1.65 trillion dollars in AI investments
summary: A report reveals that major technology companies hide a huge liability linked to spending on artificial intelligence.

title: Google Gemini approaches one billion users and its CEO announces larger base models
summary: Google's artificial intelligence assistant reaches a record number of users while the company prepares the next generation of models.

title: AMD presents EPYC Venice-X processors with up to 1,152 MB of L3 cache and 256 cores
summary: The Zen 6-based EPYC 9006 series promises big performance leaps against Intel Xeon and Nvidia Vera in high-performance computing.

title: Samsung launches Galaxy Z Fold8, Z Flip and Galaxy Watch with higher prices and generational improvements
summary: The South Korean company presents its new foldable devices and smartwatches, with pre-sale offers and significant specification changes.

title: Steam now allows buying games as gifts without needing an account
summary: Valve's new policy simplifies sending games to friends by removing the login requirement.

title: Xbox backward compatibility comes to PC with original games running via the XeFu emulator
summary: Microsoft allows playing original Xbox titles on PC using Xbox 360 emulation technology, revealing requirements and features.
